transcript:
Here's what's happening: those white branching clusters you're seeing aren't growing from dots into networks — each one arrives on screen already fully formed, mid-structure, like a sentence you've walked into halfway.

Look at the function called "spawn pre-complex form" — it builds an entire branching network, five to fifteen nodes deep, parent-child connections and all, before a single pixel ever appears. There's no moment of becoming. It's just suddenly there, wearing the look of something that grew. Even at the very start, the code stages eighty of these with "staggered false ages" — backdating their birth times so some look older than others, though none of them actually lived through anything.

The only real accumulation is the ghosting — that near-invisible fade rate, "reduced from point-oh-one-five" to "point-oh-oh-eight," so old flashes barely die before new ones land on top. That texture is the one honest history in the piece.

So everything that looks evolved is fake, and the only real memory is the residue nobody's watching.

cues.context: branching network, five to fifteen nodes deep, parent-child connections and all,
